mechanism_thesis: context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index) HURTS on these branches: the extra context dimension inflates the coverage map so naive_ctx hoards context-redundant corpus entries and spreads its mutation budget thin, while naive's flat per-edge map stays focused and resolves. naive_ctx BLOCKS, naive RESOLVES.

hypotheses.label: ctx inflates the corpus / map so naive_ctx hoards far more (context-redundant) entries than naive

params.note: count of saved corpus entries per trial; context-redundant hoarding => naive_ctx >> naive

hypotheses.prediction: corpus_count_ratio >= 1.5 (naive_ctx hoards >=1.5x the corpus naive keeps, because the same edges count as new under each call-context). A NON-ctx penalty (e.g. naive simply got luckier on byte mutation) would predict corpus_count_ratio ~ 1.0 -- no systematic corpus bloat on the blocking arm.
hypotheses.scope: corpus_queue exists ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 (data_realities). Of the 6 members, ONLY harfbuzz_5802 and harfbuzz_6124_h1 are scorable on s4; lcms/libpng/libxml2 are corpus targets on sB (the owning server arbitrates them).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: could come out corpus_count_ratio ~ 1.0; rules out 'naive just won by chance' alternative
hypotheses.g2_independent: measures corpus SIZE (the inflation mechanism), independent of the resolve pattern (which gate side each fuzzer takes) that defined the shape

hypotheses.label: a high-call-count / multi-context path at the gate is what ctx fragments (flooding) vs a shallow exact-literal gate (dilution)

params.source: per-role coverage call counts at the blocker function (e.g. png_read_IDAT_data 2750@L vs 136@W)

hypotheses.prediction: FLOODING members (deep high-iteration loop, e.g. libpng inflate loop) show call_count_lift >> 1 (naive_ctx re-enters the loop function far more, manufacturing the (ctx,edge) flood); DILUTION members (shallow 1-hop exact-literal gate, e.g. harfbuzz_6124) show call_count_lift ~ 1 -- the gate is shallow, the penalty is pure signal-splitting not loop flooding. An 'all members are the same flooding mechanism' alternative would predict call_count_lift >> 1 uniformly.
hypotheses.scope: needs per-role / coverage call-count data at the blocker function for both arms; the cards expose this for libpng (2750 vs 136) and harfbuzz, partial elsewhere.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: could come out uniform; separates flooding from dilution
hypotheses.g2_independent: call-count at the gate function is a structural property of the path, not the resolve pattern

hypotheses.label: at a SHALLOW exact-literal equality gate (not a loop, not a hoard), naive's corpus is enriched in the gate's target byte while naive_ctx's corpus is DEPLETED of it -- the per-edge reward that should pull mutation onto the winning literal is split across call-context slots so naive_ctx never assembles it

params.arms: naive_ctx (blocking) vs naive (resolving) -- NOT the registry tool's cmplog-vs-naive; a new arm pairing on the SAME byte_freq_ratio compute
params.signed_target_enrich: log2(target_frac[naive_ctx] / target_frac[naive])  -- fraction of each fuzzer's saved corpus whose byte at the gate offset == the resolving (target) value
params.decoy_enrich: log2(decoy_frac[naive_ctx] / decoy_frac[naive])  -- fraction whose byte == the blocking (decoy) value
params.gate_offsets: libxml2_6605: name[1] offset, target byte 0x6d ('m'/'M'), decoy 0x5f ('_'); harfbuzz_5802: the 0x05E9 SHIN low byte 0xe9 (paired with 0x05C2 SIN-DOT 0xc2 05), decoy = SIN-DOT-only fills (0x00/0xb9/0xc2)
params.note: operand offsets + target/decoy values come from the signature.operand_literal + card byte_diff, exactly as the registry operand_enrichment tool sources them

hypotheses.prediction: DILUTION signature: signed_target_enrich <= -0.5 (naive_ctx corpus DEPLETED of the gate target byte relative to naive -- ctx never assembled the winning literal) AND decoy_enrich >= 0 (naive_ctx pinned on the non-target byte). The obvious alternative -- 'no systematic ctx penalty, naive simply won by luck on these two gates' -- predicts signed_target_enrich ~ 0 (both corpora equally likely to carry the target byte). A pure flooding/hoarding alternative is ALSO ruled out here: these are shallow 1-hop equality gates (depth_reach call_count_lift ~ 1, corpus_size_ratio < 1.5 already observed), so the only ctx-anti signature available is corpus byte-CONTENT depletion at the gate offset, not corpus SIZE or call-depth.
hypotheses.scope: fixed-offset exact-literal value gates only (byte_freq_ratio domain). harfbuzz_5802 has an on-disk corpus on s4 (scorable). libxml2_6605 has NO corpus on s4 but libxml2 is an sB corpus target (cross-server rule: design the test valid for the branch; the owning server arbitrates -- do NOT mark decidable:false for s4 corpus absence). Needs >=3 W and >=3 L seeds in db/blockers.sqlite (the reps have them).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: could come out signed_target_enrich ~ 0 (no depletion) -> refutes dilution; the prediction is directional and could fall the other way
hypotheses.g2_independent: measures corpus byte CONTENT at the gate offset (the dilution mechanism's footprint) -- a DIFFERENT signal than corpus SIZE (corpus_size_ratio, already tried) or call-DEPTH (depth_reach, blind to context-reach here). It does NOT re-use the naive_ctx-blocks resolve pattern that defined the shape; it asks an independent question (does naive_ctx's corpus carry the literal byte at all).

decidable_caveat: PARTIALLY decidable. The 4 CONFIRMED branches re-validate under the two KEPT priors (corpus_inflation + depth_inflation). The 2 UNLABELED branches (libxml2_6605, harfbuzz_5802) failed BOTH priors because they are the SHALLOW exact-literal DILUTION sub-mechanism -- not loop-flooding (depth_reach call_count_lift ~ 1) and not corpus-hoarding (corpus_size_ratio < 1.5). The NEW hypothesis ctx_literal_corpus_depletion tests the only ctx-anti footprint left for a shallow literal gate: naive_ctx's corpus is DEPLETED of the gate target byte while naive's is enriched. This is scorable on harfbuzz_5802 (s4 corpus) and on libxml2_6605 by the sB server (libxml2 corpus lives on sB). Neither unlabeled branch is decidable:false -- a genuinely new, discriminating measurement exists for both; only the s4 server cannot score libxml2 locally, which the cross-server rule explicitly forbids treating as decidable:false.
data_realities_respected: NO seed_lineage 'ctx event' measurement -- ctx, like value_profile, is a FEEDBACK invisible in mutation_op; the only handles are what the corpus REACHES/HOARDS (size, call-depth) or CARRIES (byte content at the gate offset), never a lineage event.
corpus_queue / byte_freq_ratio scored only on corpus targets (curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 on s4; lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty on sB).

byte_freq_ratio (ctx_literal_corpus_depletion) applies ONLY to the two fixed-offset EXACT-LITERAL gates (libxml2_6605, harfbuzz_5802); it is NOT applied to the length/structural/loop members (lcms length-underflow, libpng deep-loop) -- those keep the size/depth tests, honoring the rule that byte-alignment fails on structural/length gates.
cross-server: libxml2_6605 designed test is valid for the branch; s4 cannot score it locally but sB owns and arbitrates libxml2.
notes: ROUND-1 re-design (loop-until-dry). KEPT both priors VERBATIM (ctx_corpus_inflation, ctx_depth_inflation_at_gate) so the 4 confirmed branches re-validate (superset/monotonic). ADDED one NEW hypothesis (ctx_literal_corpus_depletion) for the 2 UNLABELED branches. NEW MECHANISM: the unlabeled pair are the SHALLOW exact-literal DILUTION sub-mechanism -- ctx splits the per-edge reward for the winning literal byte across call-context slots, so naive_ctx's corpus never accumulates the target byte at the gate offset, whereas naive's flat map enriches it. This is genuinely DIFFERENT from the two refuted priors: corpus_inflation measures corpus SIZE (these gates do not hoard, ratio<1.5) and depth_inflation measures loop call-DEPTH (these gates are shallow, call_count_lift~1); the new test measures corpus byte CONTENT at the gate offset (signed_target_enrich), a signal neither prior touched. G1: the prediction is directional (depletion <= -0.5) and could come out ~0 (refuting dilution, i.e. naive won by luck). It reuses the registry's byte_freq_ratio COMPUTE but with a NEW arm pairing (naive_ctx_vs_naive instead of cmplog_vs_naive), so registry_tool=null and the orchestrator builds/parameterizes the operand variant at build time. DECIDABLE:FALSE SUB-GROUP: NONE -- both unlabeled branches have a valid discriminating test; libxml2_6605 is merely non-LOCAL to s4 (sB arbitrates), which is not a decidability failure. If at build time the byte_freq_ratio operand variant proves infeasible for the codepoint-pair gate (harfbuzz_5802 has a TWO-byte paired codepoint, not a single fixed offset), harfbuzz_5802 falls to fallback:inconclusive honestly rather than asserting a label the measurement cannot support.
